steps.title: Build the total cost
steps.content: Each canvas costs 2.15 dollars, so $c$ canvases cost $2.15c$ dollars. Each tube of paint costs 1.70 dollars, so $t$ tubes cost $1.70t$ dollars. Total spent: $2.15c + 1.70t$.

steps.title: Apply the budget
steps.content: Nora has 18 dollars, which is the maximum she can spend. The total cost must be at most 18 dollars, giving $2.15c + 1.70t \le 18$.

steps.title: Test the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $2.15c+1.70t\le18$: Correct coefficients (canvases at 2.15 dollars, paint at 1.70 dollars) and the correct $\le$ direction for a spending limit. Works.
  • Choice B — $2.15c+1.70t\ge18$: Coefficients are right, but $\ge 18$ would force Nora to spend at least 18 dollars. She only has 18 dollars. Eliminate.
  • Choice C — $1.70c+2.15t\le18$: The coefficients on $c$ and $t$ are swapped, charging the paint price for canvases. Eliminate.
  • Choice D — $1.70c+2.15t\ge18$: Both the coefficients and the inequality direction are wrong. Eliminate.
That matches Choice A — $2.15c+1.70t\le18$.
